sources.notes: Statement and constraints: each positive a_i is XORed into exactly one of P,Q,R, the values may never be pairwise distinct, and total n is at most 2e5.
Div.2 alias for the same problem; canonical reviewed identity remains 2066C.
Official contest editorial checked; the tutorial body was lazy-loaded, so the recurrence was cross-checked elsewhere.
Consulted the solution index; solutions use the prefix-XOR invariant and sparse DP keyed by the repeated value.
Used to confirm the compressed state and recurrence dp[s_{i-1}] = 3*dp[s_{i-1}] + 2*dp[s_i].
Used to cross-check transition multiplicities, initialization, and final summation.
Re-derived the transition by classifying whether the unique or one of the repeated coordinates receives a_i; brute-checked all arrays up to length 6 over values 1..4.

reference_candidates.notes: Editorial-referenced submission; direct source access failed, so it was not used as evidence.

annotation.summary: Count assignments of each a_i to P,Q,R such that at least two values remain equal after every XOR update.
annotation.constraints: 1 <= n <= 2e5, total n <= 2e5, 1 <= a_i <= 1e9, and answers are modulo 1e9+7; a sparse O(n log n) or expected O(n) DP is required.
annotation.core_idea: Let s_i=a_1 xor ... xor a_i. After i operations, P xor Q xor R=s_i. Every valid triple therefore has multiset form {s_i,x,x}. Let dp[x] count compressed valid histories, initially dp[0]=1. Since a_i>0, s_i differs from s_{i-1}. Ordinary states carry unchanged by toggling the unique s_{i-1} coordinate. Only key s_{i-1} receives extra transitions: an all-equal old state allows any of three coordinates to be toggled, while an old state whose repeated value is s_i allows either of its two equal coordinates to be toggled into s_{i-1}. Thus update dp[s_{i-1}] = 3*dp[s_{i-1}] + 2*dp[s_i] modulo MOD and leave every other entry unchanged. Sum all dp values after the final element.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) time with an ordered map and O(n) memory; expected O(n) time with a hash map.
tricks: represent a triple by its repeated value
only one DP key changes per step
multiplicity two for the equal pair

solution_variants.name: sparse-repeated-value-dp
solution_variants.summary: Maintain counts keyed by repeated value x, update only s_{i-1} using 3*dp[s_{i-1}]+2*dp[s_i], and sum the map after processing all elements.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log n) time and O(n) memory with an ordered map; expected O(n) time with a hash map.

tags.evidence: The recurrence counts valid operation sequences over compressed states modulo 1e9+7.
P xor Q xor R=s_i forces every valid triple into the form {s_i,x,x}.
The full triple is compressed to one repeated value and the known prefix XOR.
Consecutive prefix XORs s_{i-1} and s_i are exactly the keys used in every update.
Only encountered prefix-XOR values require sparse keyed storage.
Symmetry among P,Q,R produces the transition multiplicities three and two.
